title: Account Receivable Officer Job at Zurishaddai Recruitment Agency
content:
Zurishaddai Recruitment Agency was established in 2021 to provide professional Human Resources consulting and services to organizations of all sizes. We provide services, resources, and expertise in support of your Company's unique Human Resource needs. We are Human Resource professionals with over 5years of experience in this field.

Our Agency can act on your behalf as your Human Resources representative, or we can provide support and expertise to complement existing HR staff. We take the time to develop a strong working relationship with you by understanding your needs, your business and your corporate culture. With that understanding, we recommend solutions in line with your business needs and then work with you to implement them.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Receivable Officer

Job Location: Ikota, Ajah - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Duties


	Raise invoices, send statements, track customer payments
	Follow up on overdue accounts + maintain AR aging
	Reconcile customer accounts, resolve disputes
	Post receipts and update ledgers daily
	Prepare weekly/monthly AR reports for management
	Perform other tasks as assigned by the management from time to time


Key Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2+ years hands-on AR experience: invoicing, collections, reconciliation
	Must be ICAN Chartered OR ICAN Student
	Must live in close proximity to Ikota, Ajah- compulsory
	 Must be available to resume in 2-3 weeks- no exceptions
	ICAN Chartered or active ICAN student - proof required
	Strong Excel + any accounting software: Sage, QuickBooks, Tally
	Must be available to resume within 1- 2weeks


Salary and Benefit


	N219,000.00 / Month (Net)
	Staff bus - Only for staff living between Ajah and Ibeju-Lekki


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "ARO - Ikota" as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only candidates living close to Ikota and available in 1-2 weeks will be considered. Shortlisted candidates only.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Health Service Manager",
  "description": "Carpa Healthcare is a B2B medical equipment supplier that focuses on making quality healthcare and products of healthcare technologies accessible at a reasonable cost. We are a private limited liability company providing medical technology solutions to both public and private health sector operators globally. We partner with leading global innovators, developers and manufacturers of medical technology devices.
Our markets include but not limited to UK, United States, United Arab Emirates, Nigeria, Ghana, India, South Africa, Namibia, Nepal, Nigeria and many more.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health Service Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	A health services manager (or healthcare administrator) oversees the operations, finances, and personnel of healthcare facilities like hospitals, clinics, or nursing homes. They do not provide direct patient care; instead, they ensure the organization runs efficiently so that doctors and nurses can focus on treating patients.


Core Responsibilities


	Operations: Managing daily logistics, such as facility scheduling, equipment purchasing, and record-keeping.
	Finances: Creating and monitoring budgets, setting service rates, and preparing funding applications.
	Personnel: Recruiting, training, scheduling, and evaluating clinical and administrative staff.
	Compliance: Ensuring the facility adheres to evolving legal and government health regulations.
	Strategic Planning: Setting overarching goals, analyzing data to improve care quality, and preparing reports for boards of directors.


Requirements


	Leadership &amp; Communication: The ability to motivate staff, resolve conflicts, and communicate confidently with various stakeholders.
	Financial Acumen: Strong capability in budgeting and project management.


Salary
N150,000 - N350,000 / Month

Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: carpahealthcare.hr@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Health Service Manager Job at Carpa Healthcare




        
    

        
        Carpa Healthcare is a B2B medical equipment supplier that focuses on making quality healthcare and products of healthcare technologies accessible at a reasonable cost. We are a private limited liability company providing medical technology solutions to both public and private health sector operators globally. We partner with leading global innovators, developers and manufacturers of medical technology devices.
Our markets include but not limited to UK, United States, United Arab Emirates, Nigeria, Ghana, India, South Africa, Namibia, Nepal, Nigeria and many more.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health Service Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description


	A health services manager (or healthcare administrator) oversees the operations, finances, and personnel of healthcare facilities like hospitals, clinics, or nursing homes. They do not provide direct patient care; instead, they ensure the organization runs efficiently so that doctors and nurses can focus on treating patients.


Core Responsibilities


	Operations: Managing daily logistics, such as facility scheduling, equipment purchasing, and record-keeping.
	Finances: Creating and monitoring budgets, setting service rates, and preparing funding applications.
	Personnel: Recruiting, training, scheduling, and evaluating clinical and administrative staff.
	Compliance: Ensuring the facility adheres to evolving legal and government health regulations.
	Strategic Planning: Setting overarching goals, analyzing data to improve care quality, and preparing reports for boards of directors.


Requirements


	Leadership & Communication: The ability to motivate staff, resolve conflicts, and communicate confidently with various stakeholders.
	Financial Acumen: Strong capability in budgeting and project management.


Salary
N150,000 - N350,000 / Month

Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Site Engineer",
  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time | Onsite

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Site Engineer to oversee and support construction activities on-site, ensuring projects are executed according to specifications, timelines, quality standards, and safety regulations.
	The ideal candidate will work closely with project teams, contractors, and consultants to ensure the smooth delivery of construction projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise daily construction activities and ensure work is carried out according to approved drawings and specifications.
	Monitor project progress and provide regular updates on site activities.
	Coordinate contractors, subcontractors, and site workers to ensure efficient project execution.
	Conduct site inspections to ensure compliance with quality, safety, and regulatory standards.
	Review technical drawings, plans, and specifications to support construction activities.
	Assist in resolving technical issues and challenges encountered on-site.
	Ensure proper utilization of materials, equipment, and resources.
	Maintain accurate site records, reports, and project documentation.
	Monitor adherence to project schedules and escalate delays or risks where necessary.
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical input when required.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations on-site.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Civil Engineering, Building Engineering, Structural Engineering, or a related field.
	1 - 3 years of relevant experience in site engineering, construction supervision, or related roles.
	Good understanding of construction methods, materials, and site operations.
	Ability to interpret technical drawings and construction plans.
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Knowledge of AutoCAD or related engineering software is an added advantage.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced onsite environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@worknigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Site Engineer Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time | Onsite

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Site Engineer to oversee and support construction activities on-site, ensuring projects are executed according to specifications, timelines, quality standards, and safety regulations.
	The ideal candidate will work closely with project teams, contractors, and consultants to ensure the smooth delivery of construction projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise daily construction activities and ensure work is carried out according to approved drawings and specifications.
	Monitor project progress and provide regular updates on site activities.
	Coordinate contractors, subcontractors, and site workers to ensure efficient project execution.
	Conduct site inspections to ensure compliance with quality, safety, and regulatory standards.
	Review technical drawings, plans, and specifications to support construction activities.
	Assist in resolving technical issues and challenges encountered on-site.
	Ensure proper utilization of materials, equipment, and resources.
	Maintain accurate site records, reports, and project documentation.
	Monitor adherence to project schedules and escalate delays or risks where necessary.
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical input when required.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations on-site.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Civil Engineering, Building Engineering, Structural Engineering, or a related field.
	1 - 3 years of relevant experience in site engineering, construction supervision, or related roles.
	Good understanding of construction methods, materials, and site operations.
	Ability to interpret technical drawings and construction plans.
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Knowledge of AutoCAD or related engineering software is an added advantage.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced onsite environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Business Development Executive — Agricultural Produce",
  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive &mdash; Agricultural Produce

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are hiring a Business Development Executive to grow sales of our farm produce to restaurants, hotels, caterers, and other commercial buyers across Lagos.
	The role mixes desk work with frequent field activity: building new relationships, managing existing accounts, closing sales, and ensuring timely delivery of quality produce.


Key responsibilities


	Build new business relationships and manage existing accounts with restaurants, hotels, caterers, and other agro-allied buyers.
	Drive profitable sales of farm produce and meet agreed revenue targets.
	Develop and maintain a portfolio of customers through networking, cold outreach, and marketing efforts.
	Use modern technology (CRM, WhatsApp, email marketing, digital catalogues) to enhance sales and customer service.
	Meet customers on-site to assess needs and recommend suitable products or services.
	Prepare and deliver compelling product presentations and samples.
	Implement approved sales plans and field strategies to achieve targets.
	Attend industry conferences, trade expos, and local events to generate leads.
	Maintain accurate expense records for tracking and reimbursement.
	Coordinate with operations to ensure produce is delivered per customer requests and timelines.
	Confidently represent the quality of our produce and service.
	Prepare and submit weekly and monthly business development reports.
	Collect customer feedback, analyze issues, and propose solutions to improve satisfaction.
	Stay updated on industry trends and innovations; conduct market research to inform sales tactics.
	Handle company and customer information with strict confidentiality.


Skills and qualifications


	HND, or B.Sc Degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Business, Marketing, or related fields
	Prior sales or business development experience in the agro-allied industry is an advantage.
	Strong interpersonal, listening, and communication skills (verbal and written).
	Excellent negotiation and closing skills; target-oriented and self-motivated.
	Strong analytical and numerical aptitude for pricing and order tracking.
	Basic driving skills and valid driver&rsquo;s license required.
	Proficiency with mobile apps, CRM tools, and digital communication platforms.


Salary
N400,000 - N700,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@worknigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Executive — Agricultural Produce Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive — Agricultural Produce

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are hiring a Business Development Executive to grow sales of our farm produce to restaurants, hotels, caterers, and other commercial buyers across Lagos.
	The role mixes desk work with frequent field activity: building new relationships, managing existing accounts, closing sales, and ensuring timely delivery of quality produce.


Key responsibilities


	Build new business relationships and manage existing accounts with restaurants, hotels, caterers, and other agro-allied buyers.
	Drive profitable sales of farm produce and meet agreed revenue targets.
	Develop and maintain a portfolio of customers through networking, cold outreach, and marketing efforts.
	Use modern technology (CRM, WhatsApp, email marketing, digital catalogues) to enhance sales and customer service.
	Meet customers on-site to assess needs and recommend suitable products or services.
	Prepare and deliver compelling product presentations and samples.
	Implement approved sales plans and field strategies to achieve targets.
	Attend industry conferences, trade expos, and local events to generate leads.
	Maintain accurate expense records for tracking and reimbursement.
	Coordinate with operations to ensure produce is delivered per customer requests and timelines.
	Confidently represent the quality of our produce and service.
	Prepare and submit weekly and monthly business development reports.
	Collect customer feedback, analyze issues, and propose solutions to improve satisfaction.
	Stay updated on industry trends and innovations; conduct market research to inform sales tactics.
	Handle company and customer information with strict confidentiality.


Skills and qualifications


	HND, or B.Sc Degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Business, Marketing, or related fields
	Prior sales or business development experience in the agro-allied industry is an advantage.
	Strong interpersonal, listening, and communication skills (verbal and written).
	Excellent negotiation and closing skills; target-oriented and self-motivated.
	Strong analytical and numerical aptitude for pricing and order tracking.
	Basic driving skills and valid driver’s license required.
	Proficiency with mobile apps, CRM tools, and digital communication platforms.


Salary
N400,000 - N700,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Success Engineer",
  "description": "Termii is a communications platform that allows African businesses to send messages to anyone across SMS, email, voice, and instant messaging channels. With Termii businesses can verify customer identity &amp; transactions, send product notifications to customers and generate personality and engagement insights from customer transactions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Success Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

About the job


	We are seeking a Customer Success Engineer to support and delight our customers through technical troubleshooting and proactive engagement at Termii. This role is crucial in ensuring customer satisfaction, retention, and smooth onboarding of users onto our communication solutions. The ideal candidate has strong troubleshooting skills, excellent communication abilities, and a proactive attitude toward problem-solving and process improvement.


Responsibilities


	Diagnose and troubleshoot technical issues.
	Support the roll &ndash; out of new applications and features.
	Provide support in the form of procedural documentation.
	Follow up on industry trends, and suggest recommendations to improve the customer success team.&nbsp;
	Onboard, follow up and close leads/customers interested in whatsapp service to be converted to paying customers.&nbsp;
	Provide effective and timely customer technical support.
	Develop and implement innovative ideas that aid in increasing customer satisfaction and retention.&nbsp;
	Handle all vendor related issues of customers.
	Follow up and ensure all customer route/delivery pending issues are resolved.


Role Requirements


	BSc / B.A in any field.
	5+ years of proven work experience as a Technical Support representative or similar role
	Excellent communication skills
	Critical thinking/Troubleshooting skills
	Ability to work under pressure
	Strong demonstration of emotional intelligence
	Stakeholder Management and Cross functional collaboration.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@termii.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Success Engineer Job at Termii




        
    

        
        Termii is a communications platform that allows African businesses to send messages to anyone across SMS, email, voice, and instant messaging channels. With Termii businesses can verify customer identity & transactions, send product notifications to customers and generate personality and engagement insights from customer transactions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Success Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

About the job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a Customer Success Engineer to support and delight our customers through technical troubleshooting and proactive engagement at Termii. This role is crucial in ensuring customer satisfaction, retention, and smooth onboarding of users onto our communication solutions. The ideal candidate has strong troubleshooting skills, excellent communication abilities, and a proactive attitude toward problem-solving and process improvement.


Responsibilities


	Diagnose and troubleshoot technical issues.
	Support the roll – out of new applications and features.
	Provide support in the form of procedural documentation.
	Follow up on industry trends, and suggest recommendations to improve the customer success team. 
	Onboard, follow up and close leads/customers interested in whatsapp service to be converted to paying customers. 
	Provide effective and timely customer technical support.
	Develop and implement innovative ideas that aid in increasing customer satisfaction and retention. 
	Handle all vendor related issues of customers.
	Follow up and ensure all customer route/delivery pending issues are resolved.


Role Requirements


	BSc / B.A in any field.
	5+ years of proven work experience as a Technical Support representative or similar role
	Excellent communication skills
	Critical thinking/Troubleshooting skills
	Ability to work under pressure
	Strong demonstration of emotional intelligence
	Stakeholder Management and Cross functional collaboration.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
